translation.en: The car goes before I get home.

notes_md:
**trước** = *before* (in time or position). It can mean earlier than something, or in front of something.

As a time word, it usually goes **after the verb** to show something happens earlier.  

As a position word, it means “in front of” and often pairs with **ở / đứng / ngồi**.

Opposite is **sau** (after / behind) — easy pair to memorize: *trước ↔ sau*.

example.es: Comí antes de ir a dormir.
example.vi: Tôi đã ăn trước khi đi ngủ.
example.en: I ate before going to bed.

definitions.en: before; in front of; prior to.
definitions.vi: trước; ở phía trước; trước khi.

translation.en: I walk in front of the school.

notes:
trước = before (in time or position). It can mean earlier than something, or in front of something.

As a time word, it usually goes after the verb to show something happens earlier.  

As a position word, it means “in front of” and often pairs with ở / đứng / ngồi.

Opposite is sau (after / behind) — easy pair to memorize: trước ↔ sau.
exampleSentences: Em làm bài tập trước anh.
Anh gọi mẹ trước bữa tối.
Chị học bài trước giờ ngủ.
sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + làm (do) + bài tập (homework) + trước (before) + anh (you)
Anh (he) + gọi (call) + mẹ (mom) + trước (before) + bữa tối (dinner)
Chị (she) + học (study) + bài (lesson) + trước (before) + giờ ngủ (bedtime)
